what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 monochrome SVG outline icon showing three circular person nodes connected by diagonal lines, representing a team or connected group.
observations: The SVG uses a 24x24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill set to none and stroke set to currentColor.
Three path elements draw circular person nodes: one at the top and two at the lower left and lower right.
A final path draws two diagonal connector strokes between the lower nodes and the upper node area.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round caps and round joins.
standouts: The icon uses connected circular nodes instead of bust silhouettes.
The glyph inherits color through currentColor.
All visible geometry is contained in four path elements.
conventions: Uses a common icon SVG pattern: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke.
Uses round stroke caps and joins for a softened outline icon style.
Keeps the asset self-contained with inline SVG attributes and path data.
notes: A compact outline team icon built from three person nodes and two connecting strokes.
questions: Should the connected-node metaphor be interpreted as team membership, organization, or collaboration?
Is the stroke width intended to match a broader icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a team icon, and the SVG depicts a connected three-person group symbol.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable neutral team icon that adapts to surrounding UI color through currentColor.
novelty: The file represents a team as connected circular nodes rather than detailed human silhouettes.
contribution: Contributes a reusable outline glyph for representing a small connected group or team in an interface.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon with three circular person nodes connected by diagonal strokes to signify a team.
intention_for_embedding: This asset was for displaying a neutral, reusable team or connected-group icon within a design system.
